name: clear-thinking
description: Shane Parrish's Clear Thinking — decision-making toolkit teaching how to overcome the four cognitive defaults (emotion, ego, social, inertia), build strength through self-accountability, and turn ordinary moments into extraordinary results through better judgment
useCase: Understand the four defaults that hijack thinking, build self-accountability, create safeguards for weaknesses, and make better decisions aligned with what matters
knownRisks: The book is a recent (2023) publication — some frameworks are not yet tested over time
Some concepts (e.g., the four defaults) overlap significantly with other cognitive bias literature
The intelligence agency background may feel distant from everyday decision-making
